assessments.rationale: Cold stores already draw continuous multi-hundred-kW refrigeration load via dedicated MV feeder. Headroom, not presence, is the question; solvable via load-shift dispatch or minor DSO upgrade.
assessments.mitigation: DSO feeder-headroom study at site acquisition, EMS charge-window dispatch to off-peak windows, Negotiate feeder upgrade where headroom inadequate

assessments.rationale: 40-80 m2/MWh is trivial on an industrial cold-storage campus (5,000-30,000 m2 warehouse + apron + refrigeration plant). Baugenehmigung path is standard for outdoor utility equipment.
VdS/Feuerwehr clearances apply identically across all DE industrial sites. Cold stores already operate a fire-safety regime for NH3/CO2 refrigeration; adding BESS clearance is routine.
assessments.mitigation: Early Feuerwehr-Abstimmung before site layout freeze, Cluster segmentation for installations >3 MWh, Standard fire-protection concept (VdS-aligned)

assessments.rationale: Thermal-runaway containment is a product-inherent design answer, not a market-specific block. BESS sits outside the refrigerated envelope; no cold-store-specific adaptation required.
assessments.mitigation: Containerised BESS with NOVEC/aerosol suppression, Deflagration venting + cell compartmentalisation, Detection and emergency shutdown integrated with site HSE

assessments.rationale: FGW TR8 / VDE-AR-N 4110 lead time applies to every DE MV-connected asset Mein Solar delivers. Not market-specific; parallelisable with procurement and civils.
assessments.mitigation: Early Netzanschlussanfrage with DSO, Parallel Type-B plant-certificate dossier prep, Critical-path schedule with TUV/FGW-accredited certifier

assessments.rationale: LFP sourcing risk and CRMA/EU Battery Regulation compliance are portfolio-level procurement concerns identical across every market. Cold storage adds no incremental exposure.

German outdoor ambient (-15 to +35 degC typical) is well inside the -20 to +50 degC envelope. The freezing is inside the warehouse, not at the BESS container pad; container HVAC maintains internal setpoint.

10-15% AC-AC loss is a thermodynamic floor present in every BESS deployment. Cold-store peak-shaving spread (15-25 ct/kWh off-peak vs on-peak, plus Netzentgelte reduction under Paragraph 19 StromNEV) easily accommodates the loss.

250-400 EUR/kWh threshold is the generic BESS floor. Cold storage's 24/7 continuous load is an unusually good peak-shaving target (high load factor, short payback) but operator awareness is low (adoption emerging). Mitigation is commercial, not technical.
assessments.mitigation: Education-led sale with quantified peak-shaving + Netzentgelte + resilience business case, KfW 295/296 or innovation-tender financing, BESS-as-a-Service contracting to shift capex off customer balance sheet
